description.task: Implement a GradleTaskGraph class that models Gradle's task dependency system. Tasks can depend on other tasks, and when a task is executed, all its dependencies must execute first (topological order). Detect circular dependencies and throw an exception.
instructions: addTask() registers a task with no dependencies if not already present
dependsOn() adds a directed edge: 'task' requires 'dependency' to run first
executionOrder() performs a topological sort starting from targetTask
No task should appear more than once in the result, even if depended on by multiple tasks
If a circular dependency exists, throw IllegalStateException with message 'Circular dependency detected'
description.hint: Use DFS with a visited set and a recursion stack (in-progress set) to detect cycles. Add tasks to result list after all their dependencies complete (post-order).

description.task: Create a GradleScriptSimulator class that models Gradle's build script configuration using a fluent builder API. Support configuring plugins, repositories, and dependencies programmatically (mimicking what would appear in a build.gradle file).
instructions: plugin() and repository() append to their respective lists and return 'this' for chaining
dependency() stores the entry as 'configuration: gav' in the dependencies list and returns 'this'
generateSummary() returns a string in format: 'Plugins: N, Repositories: N, Dependencies: N'
All three counts reflect the number of items added at call time
The fluent API must support method chaining as shown in main()
description.hint: dependency() should do dependencies.add(configuration + ": " + gav). generateSummary() uses String.format with the three list sizes.

description.task: Implement an IncrementalBuildTracker class that simulates Gradle's incremental build feature. Track which source files have changed since the last build and determine which tasks need to be re-run (UP-TO-DATE vs NEEDS EXECUTION).
instructions: registerTask() maps a task name to its set of input file paths
recordBuild() copies all entries from fileTimestamps into lastBuildTimestamps
checkTasks() iterates over all registered tasks
A task 'NEEDS EXECUTION' if any of its input files has a currentTimestamp > lastBuildTimestamp
A task is 'UP-TO-DATE' if all input files have unchanged or missing timestamps
description.hint: For each task, iterate over its inputFiles. Look up both the lastBuildTimestamps and currentTimestamps for each file. If current > last, mark the task as NEEDS EXECUTION.
